title: garlicky indulgent portuguese fried rice with boudin-noir
description: A rich and flavorful Portuguese-inspired fried rice dish loaded with sweet boudin-noir, crispy garlic, and crunchy sugar crystals.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the boudin-noir and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sugar crystals, saffron, cinnamon stick, red pepper, onion, salt, and crushed black pepper, and cook for 2 minutes.
Push the ingredients to one side of the pan, and add more coconut oil if necessary.
Crack the eggs into the empty side of the pan, and cook until set, about 2-3 minutes.
Scramble the eggs, then mix them with the other ingredients.
Add the cooked rice to the pan, and stir-fry everything together for about 5 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the fried rice hot, garnished with additional sugar crystals and garlic if desired.
